prevalence.source: European Thyroid Association (ETA) / European epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; detection improved with routine thyroid testing

burden.mortalityRate: Low; mainly via cardiovascular complications in untreated cases

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3; etiology by TRAb and uptake imaging where indicated; ETA-aligned pathways

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; high testing rates in symptomatic and risk groups
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Generally high with structured endocrine care; relapse rates remain for Graves disease

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad access to thyroid labs and imaging in specialist settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antithyroid drugs, radioiodine, and surgery based on etiology and patient preference; beta-blockers for symptom control
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in definitive therapy preferences between countries and management of comorbidities in older adults
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong guideline infrastructure (ETA) and widespread access to diagnostic and definitive therapies
